name: Free social media link-in-bio tools: Compile all social media links
description: A short and quick guide on how to build your link in bio page for social. We’ve reviewed a list of helpful tools that will best serve this purpose.
main_title: Free social media link-in-bio tools: Compile all social media links
richtext:
Having a single link to showcase all your social media profiles and essential content can be of great use to brands and creators. Link-in-bio tools allow creators, businesses, and influencers to optimize their social media presence by providing an easily accessible hub for their audience. This blog explores the concept of link-in-bio tools, how they can help, and some of the best free tools available to compile all your links in one place.

What is link-in-bio?

A link-in-bio refers to a clickable link that users can link to all social media accounts and other assets, especially on platforms like Instagram, TikTok, and Twitter. Since most social platforms limit users to one external link in their bios, link-in-bio tools help maximize this single link by redirecting visitors to a custom landing page containing multiple links.

‍

Source: Tagshop

For example, if you’re a content creator, your link-in-bio might include links to your YouTube channel, blog, latest projects, or an online store. It’s a simple yet effective way to consolidate all your key resources in one place.

How can a link-in-bio tool help?

A link-in-bio tool makes it super easy for your customers to find all relevant links and important information about your brand in one place. It’s kinda like having a website to link all social media pages and other channels for your brand.

  • These tools act as a hub, allowing users to showcase all their social platforms, websites, and resources in one easily accessible place.
  • Direct followers to specific campaigns, products, or pages without juggling multiple URLs.
  • Many link-in-bio tools offer analytics to track clicks and engagement, providing valuable insights into audience behavior.
  • Customizable link pages can reflect your personal or business brand, ensuring a cohesive and professional online presence.

How to build your link page?

Creating a link page is straightforward, especially with user-friendly tools. Follow these steps:

  1. Choose the Right Tool: Select a link-in-bio tool that suits your needs and budget (free or paid options are available).
  2. Sign Up: Register for an account and connect it to your social media profiles.
  3. Customize Your Page: Add your links, upload images, and choose a design that aligns with your branding.
  4. Optimize Links: Prioritize important links, ensuring they’re placed prominently on the page.
  5. Test Your Page: Before sharing, ensure all links are functional and the page looks good on desktop and mobile devices.
  6. Add the Link to Your Bio: Copy the provided URL and paste it into the bio section of your social media profiles.

Best link-in-bio tools to bring all your social media links in one place

These are some of the commonly used link page tools that you can try. Depending on what features you’re looking for, you can check out some of the top ones as we mentioned.

1. Metricool

Metricool offers a comprehensive social media management platform, including a link-in-bio feature called Bio Link. It allows users to create visually appealing link pages with performance analytics.

‍

Source: Metricool

Price: Free plan available; starter plans start at $18/month.

Pros:

  • Easy-to-use interface.
  • Advanced analytics for tracking clicks.
  • Integrates with social media scheduling tools.

Cons:

  • Limited customization options in the free version.
  • Best suited for users already leveraging Metricool’s broader platform.

2. FeedLink

FeedLink by EmbedSocial creates clickable image-based link pages, ideal for Instagram and Pinterest users. It’s perfect for showcasing product galleries and portfolios.

‍

Source: Feedlink

Price: Free plan available; pro plans starts at $6 per month billed annually.

Pros:

  • Image-centric design for visual appeal.
  • Supports shoppable links.

Cons:

  • Limited text-based link options.
  • Higher pricing compared to similar tools.

3. LinkTree

One of the most popular link-in-bio tools, LinkTree offers simple yet effective options for building a multi-link page.

‍

Source: Dribbble

Price: Free plan available; starter plans start at $5/month billed monthly.

Pros:

  • Intuitive and beginner-friendly.
  • Basic analytics included in the free plan.
  • Supports integrations with payment platforms.

Cons:

  • Limited design customization in the free version.
  • Heavily branded with LinkTree’s logo unless upgraded.

4. Lnk.Bio

Lnk.Bio provides a minimalist interface for creating simple and functional link pages. It’s ideal for users seeking a no-frills solution.

‍

Source: Lnk.bio

Price: Free plan available; lifetime premium access for a one-time fee of $10

Pros:

  • Affordable lifetime pricing.
  • Straightforward setup process.

Cons:

  • Limited advanced features like analytics.
  • Basic design options.

5. Compiled Social

Compiled Social emphasizes personalization, offering users the ability to create highly customizable link pages.

‍

Source: Compiled Social

‍

Price: Free plan available; premium plans start at $10/month.

Pros:

  • Extensive customization options.
  • Offers detailed analytics.

Cons:

  • May require more setup time.
  • Some advanced features are locked behind the paywall.

6. Milkshake

Milkshake allows users to create mini-websites optimized for mobile, making it perfect for influencers and small businesses.

Source: Milkshake

Price: Free to use.

Pros:

  • Mobile-first design.
  • Highly visual templates.

Cons:

  • Limited analytics.
  • Best suited for users focused on mobile audiences.

7. Tap Bio

Tap Bio is a card-based link-in-bio tool that organizes content into multiple slides, offering a unique browsing experience.

‍

Source: Tap Bio

Price: Free plan available; premium plans start at $3/month for up to 3 cards.

Pros:

  • Unique card-based design.
  • Easy navigation for visitors.

Cons:

  • Slight learning curve for setup.
  • Limited customization in free tier.

Also read: Top 100 social media management tools (Free & Paid)

‍

Sharing and promoting your link page

Once you’ve created your link page, the next step is to share and promote it effectively:

  • Add It to All Profiles: Update the bio section of all your social media platforms with the link.
  • Include It in Email Signatures: Add the link to your email signature for professional correspondence.
  • Promote It in Posts: Regularly share posts reminding followers to check the link in your bio.
  • Collaborate with Influencers: Encourage influencers or brand ambassadors to share your link page.
  • Use QR Codes: Generate a QR code that directs users to your link page and include it in printed materials or product packaging.

‍

Synup Social helps you manage multiple social media accounts in one place

Synup Social helps you take care of your social media content across multiple platforms and brands. Synup is also a no-BS social schedule tool that gives you the best value for your price, without overwhelming you with too many features that you won’t ever end up using.

Book a demo today to see the tool in action

‍

I’ll leave you with this…

Link-in-bio tools are no longer optional in today’s digital landscape—they’re essential. Whether you choose a classic option like LinkTree or experiment with a visually unique tool like Milkshake, the right link-in-bio platform can enhance your branding, improve audience engagement, and simplify how you share your content. Take the first step, build your link page, and watch as it transforms your social media presence.

‍
